ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Crudo e Rucola pizza; mapped to the broader pizza food product class as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pizza as a category; no specific code exists for prosciutto crudo and rocket variant. Broader code used as defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific pizza variant; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical restaurant serving of Crudo e Rucola pizza is approximately 300 g, representing a whole thin-crust pizza of standard diameter (28–30 cm) as commonly served in Italian-style restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — Crudo E Rucola (G00010909)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010909 for Crudo E Rucola. The HPB record reports energy 42 kcal/100g, protein 0g, fat 0.25g, carbohydrate 10.36g, sugar 9.94g, sodium 3mg, dietary fibre 0g, potassium 5mg, calcium 1mg, magnesium 0mg, iron 0.02mg, cholesterol 0mg. These values are anomalous for a pizza dish containing prosciutto, mozzarella, and Parmesan — the HPB record likely reflects only the rocket/salad component or a partial data entry. Values are preserved as reported from the approved source; anomalies are documented in notes. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and extended micronutrients are not reported in the HPB record and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Pizza, cheese, thin and crispy (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Crudo e Rucola pizza in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 60 is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source entry: thin-crust cheese pizza (GI ~60, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The pizza base and tomato sauce are the primary glycemic drivers; prosciutto and rocket contribute negligible carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × carbohydrate per serving (31.1 g from 300 g × 10.36 g/100g) / 100 = 18.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Prosciutto crudo (Parma ham) is a dry-cured meat with very high sodium content (typically 1,500–2,000 mg/100g); the HPB-reported sodium of 3 mg/100g appears to reflect only the rocket leaf component and substantially underestimates the true dish sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Crudo e Rucola pizza due to the very high sodium load from prosciutto crudo and Parmesan cheese; if consumed, request a reduced-ham portion and avoid adding salt or high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: The pizza base contributes moderate glycemic load (estimated GL ~18.7 per serving); the anomalously high sugar value (9.94 g/100g) in the HPB record suggests a sweetened dressing component that would further elevate postprandial glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one or two slices, request unsweetened olive oil and lemon dressing in place of any sweetened dressing, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to moderate the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Prosciutto crudo is a processed cured meat high in saturated fat and sodium; regular consumption of processed cured meats is associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk per WHO and AHA guidelines.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Crudo e Rucola pizza as an occasional food only, limit to one or two slices per sitting, and choose preparations with minimal added cheese and no sweetened dressing to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Processed cured meats such as prosciutto are pro-inflammatory due to high sodium, saturated fat, and advanced glycation end-products; added sugar from the dressing further promotes inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit or avoid Crudo e Rucola pizza and request plain olive oil and lemon dressing to reduce pro-inflammatory dietary load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crudo e Rucola is an Italian-style pizza topped with Parma ham (prosciutto crudo), fresh rocket leaves (arugula), Parmesan shavings, mozzarella cheese, and tomato sauce. The pizza base is baked until crisp, and the prosciutto and rocket are typically added after baking to preserve their delicate texture and flavour. The dish delivers a contrast of savoury, salty cured ham against the peppery bitterness of rocket and the rich creaminess of melted mozzarella, finished with the nutty sharpness of Parmesan. It is a popular menu item in Italian-style restaurants in Singapore and is commonly served as a main course at lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, the dish is a source of protein from prosciutto and cheese, moderate carbohydrate from the pizza base, and significant sodium from the cured meat and cheese components. The HPB Singapore database record (G00010909) reports anomalously low protein (0 g/100g) and sodium (3 mg/100g), which are inconsistent with the known composition of a pizza containing prosciutto crudo (typically 25–28 g protein/100g and 1,500–2,000 mg sodium/100g); these values likely reflect only the rocket leaf or dressing component rather than the full dish. Clinically, the dish is of concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to the high sodium and saturated fat content of prosciutto crudo and Parmesan. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium) based on the thin-crust pizza base, with a glycemic load of approximately 18.7 per standard 300 g serving.

confidence_reason: The HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010909 is an approved primary source for Singapore food records, but the reported nutrient values (0 g protein, 3 mg sodium/100g, 9.94 g sugar/100g) are internally inconsistent with the known composition of a pizza containing prosciutto crudo, mozzarella, and Parmesan. The values appear to reflect only the rocket leaf or dressing component rather than the full dish. Confidence is therefore set to medium: the source is approved and the record is preserved as reported, but the nutritional data does not represent the complete dish as described.
notes: ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: protein_g is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source record G00010909. This is anomalous for a dish containing prosciutto crudo (typically 25–28 g protein/100g) and mozzarella cheese. The zero value is preserved as reported from the approved HPB source but is flagged as likely reflecting only the rocket leaf or dressing component rather than the full dish. This is not naturally zero; it is a suspected partial data entry confirmed zero per HPB Foodo G00010909. Fat_g of 0.25 g/100g is similarly anomalous for a pizza with cured meat and two cheese types but is confirmed as reported per HPB Foodo G00010909. Dietary_fibre_g of 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB source for this record. Cholesterol_mg of 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB source for this record, which is also anomalous given the cheese and cured meat content. Magnesium_mg of 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB source for this record. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1 because these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010909 and no supplementary approved-source data was available to complete them without risking unsupported values. DATA ANOMALY NOTE: The HPB record G00010909 reports energy 42 kcal/100g, protein 0g, fat 0.25g, carbohydrate 10.36g, sugar 9.94g, sodium 3mg — values consistent with a sweetened rocket salad or dressing rather than a complete pizza dish. The description lists Parma Ham, Rocket Leaves, Parmesan Shaving, Tomato Sauce, and Mozzarella Cheese. The nutritional data is preserved as reported from the approved source per Project Veritas source policy, but users and clinicians should be aware that the true nutritional profile of this dish as served is substantially different from the HPB record values. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_00002572 (pizza food product) and FoodEx2 A00LC (Pizza) are used as broader-class anchors; no exact codes exist for this specific pizza variant.
